digest.component_or_area: project infrastructure serial auto-lander (Gerrit + GitHub Actions)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions race to land changes on fast-forward-only main, forcing manual rebase-and-retry that burns CI runs and agent attention.
Build a serial auto-lander that rebases the front Autosubmit change/chain to tip, awaits fresh Verified, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA with zero further agent attention.
The agent contract is a single land command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged/needs_rebase/ci_failed/review_failed) so agents never correlate Gerrit votes or labels themselves.
Landing logic must stay in project infrastructure (infra/ + CLI) and add no Gerrit-specific code to rebar core, keeping rebar platform-agnostic.
Security invariants preserved: non-gating requester-votable Autosubmit label scoped to Contributors, re-verify submittability on the tested SHA before submit, two-vote gate never weakened.
Cross-stack batching (Phase 2) is rejected/deferred to bounded-speculation idea; the stack is the atomic unit and failures hand the whole stack back to its owning agent.
